title: Cost of Capital: What is the expected return on equity if the firm...
summary: Calculate the expected return using CAPM with beta, risk-free rate, and market return.
prompt: What is the expected return on equity if the firm has a beta of 1.2, a risk-free rate of 2%, and a market return of 10%?
solution: Expected Return = Risk-Free Rate + Beta × (Market Return - Risk-Free Rate) = 2% + 1.2 × (10% - 2%) = 2% + 1.2 × 8% = 2% + 9.6% = 11.6%. This return reflects the risk-adjusted expected return based on market conditions.
